title: luzula australasica subsp. dura
intro: Luzula australasica subsp. dura, also known as the Tasmanian woodrush, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Juncaceae. It is a perennial herb with narrow, grass-like leaves and small yellowish-brown flowers. The medicinal properties of Luzula australasica subsp. dura include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation and infection, and it has been used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs.
